channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Judge again orders Trump administration to return man mistakenly deported to an El Salvador prison
item.description: GREENBELT, Md. (AP) — A federal judge is for the second time ordering the Trump administration to return a Maryland man who was mistakenly sent to a notorious prison in El Salvador, blasting the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Researchers developing at-home smell test to detect Alzheimer’s
item.description: A smell test is less invasive and more accessible than other methods like PET scans, lumbar punctures or blood tests.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, KRON4

item.title: Florida charter captain arrested after viral altercation with young fisherman
item.description: Police in Punta Gorda spoke out about the incident Saturday, saying "this type of behavior will not be tolerated.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, Fox 5

item.title: Protesters from New York to Alaska assail Trump and Musk in 'Hands Off!' rallies
item.description: Crowds of people angry about the way President Donald Trump is running the country marched and rallied in scores of American cities Saturday in the biggest day of demonstrations yet by an opposition movement trying to regain its momentum after the shock of the Republican’s first weeks in office.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: DOJ lawyer who criticized admin over Maryland man's deportation placed on leave
item.description: Erez Reuveni, one of the DOJ's senior immigration lawyers, has been placed on leave after criticizing the Trump administration over mistakenly deporting a Maryland man to a prison in El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: At least 16 dead in flooding, tornadoes as storms slash parts of US
item.description: Many of the impacted areas already are heavily waterlogged by days of severe storms that spawned deadly tornadoes.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Jaguar and Land Rover carmaker pauses shipments to US over Trump tariffs
item.description: The company that makes Jaguar and Land Rover vehicles said on Saturday it would pause shipments to the U.S., days after President Donald Trump’s tariffs took effect.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: State Supreme Courts have become an electoral battleground. But some states choose a different path
item.description: The recent election that determined control of the Wisconsin Supreme Court saw millions of dollars flooding in from billionaires, featured attack ads and drew national political attention.
item.dc:creator: Christine Fernando And John Hanna, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: GOP senators back crackdown on foreign students
item.description: Republican senators are brushing off concerns that high-profile arrests and deportations of foreign students may ostracize a group that is a major contributor to the U.S. economy. International students injected $43.8 billion into the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Houston rallies to beat Duke 70-67 in the Final Four, will face Florida for the NCAA title
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — Sorry guys, “Phi Slama Jama” is already taken. How ’bout something simple, like “Comeback Kids.” “National champs” might be a possibility, too.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: At least 16 dead in flooding and tornadoes as storms slash from Texas to Ohio
item.description: DYERSBURG, Tennessee. (AP) — Another round of torrential rain and flash flooding came Saturday for parts of the South and Midwest already heavily waterlogged by days of severe storms that also spawned deadly tornadoes. Forecasters warned that rivers in some places would continue to rise for days.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and OBED LAMY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Thousands of Ukrainian civilians are still held by Russia with uncertain hope of release
item.description: When she heard her front door open almost two years ago, Kostiantyn Zinovkin’s mother thought her son had returned home because he forgot something. Instead, men in balaclavas burst into the apartment in Melitopol, a southern Ukrainian city occupied by Russian forces.
item.dc:creator: DASHA LITVINOVA and HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Amanda Knox gives warning to students wanting to study abroad, 10 years after being acquitted of murder
item.description: Amanda Knox, a married mother of two, gives advice to students and their parents on whether study abroad is a safe option in 2025. She has a new book, "Free: My Search for Meaning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Las Vegas Sphere launching 'experience' around classic film
item.description: Sphere, the famed immersive Las Vegas entertainment arena, is set to debut two new "Sphere Experiences" later this year — and one is based on a beloved classic motion picture.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, NewsNation

item.title: Texas county that swung to Trump grapples with immigration crackdown after bakery is targeted
item.description: A recent immigration enforcement action that resulted in the arrests of employees and owners at a Texas bakery has shocked residents of the town of Los Fresnos.
item.dc:creator: Valerie Gonzalez, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tariffs will make sneakers, jeans and almost everything Americans wear cost more, trade groups warn
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Sending children back to school in new sneakers, jeans and T-shirts is likely to cost U.S. families significantly more this fall if the bespoke tariffs President Donald Trump put on leading exporters take effect as planned, American industry groups warn.
item.dc:creator: ANNE D'INNOCENZIO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Several University of Massachusetts international students have visas revoked
item.description: Five international students at the University of Massachusetts Amherst are the latest in the country to have their student visas revoked by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump goes all in with bet that the heavy price of tariffs will pay off for Americans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Not even 24 hours after his party lost a key Wisconsin race and underperformed in Florida, President Donald Trump followed the playbook that has defined his political career: He doubled down. Trump’s move Wednesday to place stiff new tariffs on imports from nearly all U.S.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and STEVE PEOPLES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Al Green says he’ll present articles of impeachment against Trump in next 30 days
item.description: Green, who vowed to bring articles of impeachment against Trump roughly two months ago, argued that Trump does not “deserve the office” he holds.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: Baby boomers are buying more homes than millennials
item.description: Older buyers — who are more likely to purchase homes with cash — have been less affected by elevated mortgage rates.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KTLA 5

item.title: NY public schools tell Trump administration they won't comply with DEI order
item.description: New York state officials have told the Trump administration that they will not comply with its demands to end diversity, equity and inclusion practices in public schools, despite the administration's threats to terminate federal education funding.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: St. Louis family's prank leads to lobster's prolonged life
item.description: What started off as an April Fool’s prank turned into something more than what Brea Stenzel, her family and a lobster were expecting.
item.dc:creator: Daesha Gear, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Budget calculator: Calculate your monthly budget using the 50/30/20 rule
item.description: The 50/30/20 rule is a popular budgeting technique. Use this calculator to figure out how to spend your money.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Teams battle it out in NCAA Women's Final Four
item.description: The South Carolina Gamecocks are headed back to the NCAA Championship game to fight for their second straight national title.
item.dc:creator: Marilyn Parker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump backs Luna push for House parental proxy voting
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) has been scrambling to break an impasse over the push for proxy voting, after Luna joined with more than 200 Democrats and nine Republicans to use a procedure known as a discharge petition to circumvent leadership and force a vote on the matter.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump approval slips to lowest point in second term: Survey
item.description: President Trump’s approval rating slipped to its lowest point during his second White House term amid his handling of the economy and the recent Houthi Signal chat leak, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Ford offering employee pricing to all shoppers in wake of Trump tariffs
item.description: The campaign comes as Trump has rolled out a fresh package of tariffs, impacting nearly every country in the world.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: Transgender student's arrest for violating Florida bathroom law is thought to be a first
item.description: A transgender college student declared “I am here to break the law” before entering a women’s restroom at the Florida State Capitol and being led out in handcuffs by police.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: White House nears deadline to avoid TikTok ban, potential deal in the works
item.description: The Trump administration is racing against the clock to secure a deal that would keep TikTok operational in the United States ahead of Saturday’s deadline.
item.dc:creator: Raquel Martin, WGN 9

item.title: When heavy rainfall threatens flooding, you can protect yourself. Here's how
item.description: Extreme weather is dangerous weather, and that's particularly true for the heavy rainfall events that experts say are becoming more frequent with climate change. The powerful storms can pose threats ranging from falling limbs to downed power lines to drowning.
item.dc:creator: ALEXA ST. JOHN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: This is what forecasters mean when they talk about a 100-year flood
item.description: Weather forecasters sometimes warn of storms that unleash rains so unusual they are described as 100-year or even 500-year floods. Here’s what to know about how scientists determine how extreme a flood is and how common these extreme events are becoming.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA O'MALLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: University of Minnesota student who was detained by ICE sues for immediate release
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — A University of Minnesota graduate business student who's being held by Immigration and Customs Enforcement is suing for his immediate release, saying his arrest violated his rights and he's been given little explanation for why he's being held.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4
